title: Chemical fixation of Solarion arienae for transmission electron microscopy
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3b708","text":"This is an optimized version of the protocol for standard chemical fixation for transmission electron microscopy that was used for the fixation of the culture of Solarion arienae.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":162,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Centrifuge a well-grown culture at 1250 g at 4 °C.
Immerse pellet with 2,5% glutaraldehyde fixative in 0,1M cacodylate buffer solution for 1h. Work on ice.
Post-fix with 2% OsO4 in 0,1 M cacodylate buffer for 1h, on ice
Wash fixed samples in distilled water, 3 times.
Dehydrate samples in ethanol series 30%, 50%, 70%, 80%, 90%, 95%, (100% 3 times)
Impregnate the dehydrated sample with acetone 1:1 ethanol solution.
Transfer the sample to (EMbed 812) resin. Repeat 3 times every 4h. (can be left longer or overnight)
Polymerize samples embedded in resin at 70 °C for 48h.
Cut sections. For Solarion, 80nm thick sections were cut with a diamond knife on an Ultracut E ultramicrotome (Reichert).
post-contrast with uranyl acetate and lead citrate.
creator: Marek Valt
